meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve no version control (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no version control. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Antigravity Project Template: Solve Version Control for Students
content.seo_description: Master version control with the Antigravity project template. An intermediate guide for students to build professional apps using Claude Code effectively.
content.answer_block: The Antigravity project template acts as your foundational architecture, enforcing strict version control and modularity. By integrating Claude Code, you automate repository initialization, branching strategies, and commit history, effectively eliminating the chaos of unmanaged codebases. This setup transforms your student projects into professional-grade portfolios ready for production deployment.
content.intro:
You have spent hours crafting the perfect logic, only to realize your project is a sprawling, untracked mess. Without version control, you are one accidental keystroke away from total system failure. The sinking feeling of a corrupted file that cannot be recovered is the rite of passage every developer fears, but it is also the threshold between amateur coding and professional engineering.

Today, we stop the bleeding. By adopting the Antigravity project template, you are not just saving files; you are implementing a professional deployment pipeline. This is where you transition from writing code to building scalable software systems.

steps.title: Initialize Environment with Antigravity
steps.description: Establish a standardized repository structure that enforces best practices from the first commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize an Antigravity project template with strict Git integration and a structured directory layout for a web-based SaaS application.

steps.title: Configure Automated Branching
steps.description: Ensure every feature development happens in a safe, isolated environment before merging.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up a git-flow branching policy within the Antigravity config to enforce feature-branching for all new development tasks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "configure git-flow and enforce branch protection rules"

steps.title: Automate Commit Hygiene
steps.description: Use Claude Code to generate semantic commit messages that explain the 'why' behind every code change.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a post-commit hook that analyzes code changes and suggests semantic commit messages based on Conventional Commits.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "create a husky hook for semantic commit validation"

steps.title: Sync Local State to Remote
steps.description: Bridge the gap between your local development machine and your GitHub/GitLab remote repository.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Sync the current Antigravity workspace to a new remote repository and verify the deployment pipeline integrity.

steps.title: Implement Continuous Integration
steps.description: Automate testing to ensure your version control history remains 'green' and deployable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a CI workflow in the Antigravity template that runs linting and unit tests on every pull request.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "generate github actions workflow for automated testing"

pro_tips: Use git-ignore templates specific to your framework to avoid bloating your repo with node_modules or environment secrets.
Always commit your progress before asking Claude Code to refactor large sections of the codebase to allow for easy reverts.
Treat your commit history as a documentation log; future employers will read it to understand your problem-solving process.
Use atomic commits—one feature, one commit—to keep the history clean and readable for peer reviewers.
Enable 'Branch Protection' on your remote host to prevent force-pushes to your main branch, ensuring your history is immutable.
content.next_steps: Once your repository is stable, focus on deploying your first feature branch to a staging environment and sharing the link with a mentor for a code review.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the blueprint to stop losing work and start building a career. Don't let your code sit unmanaged on your local machine. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
